title: Incremental SSE streaming for OpenAI provider
purpose: The OpenAI provider currently buffers the entire HTTP response body before parsing SSE events (response.text().await then parse_sse_bytes). The consumer sees a stream, but only after the full response completes — losing the real-time token-by-token streaming that makes agent work observable. This brief replaces buffered reading with incremental chunk processing via reqwest bytes_stream().
task: Create a stateful SseParser that accepts partial byte chunks and yields complete SSE events as they become available. Replace response.text().await with response.bytes_stream() in SenderProvider::execute. Feed chunks through SseParser incrementally, sending mapped ProviderEvents through the channel as each SSE frame completes. Handle frames that span chunk boundaries. Testing: unit tests with deliberately fragmented SSE payloads.

requirements.title: Stateful incremental SSE parser
requirements.spec: SseParser SHALL accept arbitrary byte chunks and yield complete SSE events as frames become available. It SHALL correctly handle frames split across chunk boundaries.
acceptance: SseParser struct in crates/norn/src/provider/openai/sse.rs
SseParser::new() creates empty parser state
SseParser::feed(&mut self, chunk: &[u8]) -> Vec<SseEvent> yields zero or more complete events
Handles event: and data: fields spanning chunk boundaries
Handles double-newline frame delimiter split across chunks
Handles multiple complete frames in a single chunk
Handles empty chunks (yields nothing)
Handles UTF-8 codepoints split across chunks
Test: feed 'data: {"text":"hel' then 'lo"}\n\n', verify one complete event

Test: feed 'data: a

data: b

' in one chunk, verify two events
Test: feed empty chunk between valid chunks, verify no crash and events still parse

requirements.title: Replace buffered reading with bytes_stream
requirements.spec: SenderProvider::execute SHALL use response.bytes_stream() instead of response.text().await. Each chunk is fed to SseParser and resulting events are sent immediately through the channel.
acceptance: response.text().await removed from SenderProvider::execute
response.bytes_stream() used instead — reqwest stream feature already enabled
Each chunk fed to parser.feed(), resulting events mapped via map_sse_event and sent via tx.send
Events arrive at the consumer as they are parsed, not after full response completes
HTTP errors (non-200 status) still detected before streaming begins
Connection drops mid-stream produce a ProviderError::StreamInterrupted
Existing parse_sse_bytes function retained for test utilities but no longer used in production path
Test: mock HTTP response delivering 3 events in 3 separate chunks, verify consumer receives each before the next arrives

requirements.title: Rate limiter interaction with streaming
requirements.spec: The token-bucket rate limiter SHALL acquire before the streaming request begins, not after. 429 responses SHALL still be handled with Retry-After header parsing.
acceptance: Rate limiter acquire() called before HTTP request, same as current behavior
429 responses detected from status code before bytes_stream() is consumed
Retry-After header parsed and respected on 429
Rate limiter is not held during streaming — only during the initial request
Test: verify 429 with Retry-After retries correctly with incremental streaming

boundaries: SHALL NOT modify the ProviderEvent enum or Provider trait
SHALL NOT modify the consumer-side stream interface (ProviderStream stays the same)
SHALL NOT implement SSE for the Claude Runner adapter (it uses its own streaming format)
SHALL NOT add todo!() or unimplemented!() macros anywhere
verification: cargo test -p norn — all provider tests pass
SseParser correctly handles fragmented frames — verified by 3+ unit tests
Events stream incrementally (not buffered) — verified by test with multi-chunk delivery
